raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The state Office of Suicide Prevention will provide strategic guidance regarding best prevention practices.",
    "The new office will focus resources on high-risk groups such as youth, Native American youth, older adults, veterans, and LGBTQ people.",
    "The state will conduct evaluations of regional and state suicide prevention policies.",
    "Data will be reviewed to identify opportunities to reduce suicide, including documenting aborted suicide attempts and crisis service interventions.",
    "The office will marshal insights and energy from medical professionals, scientists, and public health experts to address the suicide crisis.",
    "Information will be disseminated to achieve statewide progress, including campaigns to reach populations with high suicide rates.",
    "Calls to mental health hotlines have increased significantly during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "A reported study indicated that for every percentage point increase in the unemployment rate, there is an associated increase in the suicide rate.",
    "Research suggests that the number of lives lost to suicide will increase as a result of COVID-19.",
    "The COVID-19 pandemic has exposed and worsened the epidemic of youth suicide, which is the second most prevalent cause of death for adolescents.",
    "Suicide is the second leading cause of death among young people ages 15 to 24.",
    "One in six high school students reported seriously considering ending their lives, as per the CDC's data.",
    "Over one in 12 high school students reported attempted suicide according to CDC data.",
    "In 2014, 3,575 children ages 5 to 20 were hospitalized for non-fatal self-inflicted injuries in California."
  ]
}
```
